goal: Profiler L4: canonical coverage report mapping source columns to schema version fields with gap analysis

acceptance_criteria.description: CoverageReport model in models/types.go: report_id (UUID), profiling_run_id, schema_version_id (FK to schema_version), total_canonical_fields (int), mapped_fields (int — canonical fields with at least one source column mapped), unmapped_fields (int), coverage_pct (float64 — mapped_fields / total_canonical_fields), auto_mapped_count (int — mappings with confidence >= 0.8 from name/type similarity), review_required_count (int — mappings with confidence 0.5-0.79), no_match_count (int), field_details (JSONB — array of CoverageFieldDetail), created_at. CoverageFieldDetail: canonical_entity, field_name, data_type, is_required, status ('MAPPED'|'AUTO_MAPPED'|'REVIEW_REQUIRED'|'UNMAPPED'), source_candidates (array of {source_table, source_column, confidence, match_reason}).

Migration 055_coverage_report.sql creates migration.coverage_report table: UUID PK, profiling_run_id FK, schema_version_id FK to schema_version, count columns INT, coverage_pct NUMERIC(5,2), field_details JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now(). RLS Tier B via profiling_run — engagement — tenant_id. UNIQUE(profiling_run_id, schema_version_id) — one report per run per schema version.

L4Executor in worker/profile_l4_executor.go implementing worker.Executor (matching L1/L2/L3 pattern). Step 1: Load active schema_version and its fields via db.GetActiveSchemaVersionID and db.GetBaselineFields. Step 2: Load all source_column records from the profiling run (from L1/L2). Step 3: For each canonical field, find source column candidates by: exact name match (confidence 0.95), name similarity after removing common prefixes/suffixes (0.8), data type compatibility scoring (0.6). Step 4: Classify each canonical field: MAPPED if existing field_mapping exists, AUTO_MAPPED if best candidate confidence >= 0.8, REVIEW_REQUIRED if best candidate 0.5-0.79, UNMAPPED if no candidates above 0.5. Step 5: Updates profiling_run aggregate columns: total_canonical_fields, auto_mapped_count, review_required_count, unmapped_count, overall_coverage_pct.

L4Executor registered in main.go with job_type='profile_l4'. Updates profiling run status to RUNNING_L4 at start, level_reached=4 on completion. WebSocket broadcast: profiling_l4_completed with engagement_id, coverage_pct, auto_mapped_count, unmapped_count. Stores report via db.InsertCoverageReport. GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/profiling/{runId}/coverage returns the coverage report with field_details. GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/profiling/{runId}/coverage/gaps returns only UNMAPPED and REVIEW_REQUIRED fields — the primary action list for analysts.

critical: Coverage report references a specific schema_version_id — reproducible against any schema version, not just 'active'
Name matching uses normalization (lowercase, strip prefixes like 'fk_', 'idx_') — not raw string comparison
Coverage percentages use total_canonical_fields as denominator — required fields weighted equally to optional
L4 requires L2 completion (source_column data) and active schema_version
high: Candidate scoring is deterministic and documented — no AI/ML in the path
Existing field_mapping records are checked first — MAPPED status reflects human-approved mappings
UNIQUE(profiling_run_id, schema_version_id) — re-running L4 against same version overwrites previous report
profiling_run aggregate columns updated — coverage_handler.go can serve from profiling_run without joining coverage_report
medium: source_candidates array in field_details limited to top 5 per canonical field — prevents JSONB bloat
Coverage gaps endpoint (?status=UNMAPPED,REVIEW_REQUIRED) is the primary analyst workflow entry point
